mappings.rationaleAI: Blood composition including hemoglobin, plasma, red cells, white cells, and platelets directly addresses circulating fluid media (B.2), plasma (B.2.b), formed elements (B.2.c), lymphocytes (B.2.d), vertebrate circulatory systems (B.3.b), and biodynamics of circulation (B.3.e).
Energy supply and homeostasis address respiration (A), the human respiratory system (A.7), cardiovascular function (B.3.f), and excretory processes (C).
Structures for breathing and organs of the chest address the human respiratory system (A.7), and lymphatic system anatomy relates to the human cardiovascular system (B.3.f).
Diversity of animal phyla with varied respiratory and circulatory structures addresses respiratory organs (A.3) and invertebrate circulatory systems (B.3.a).
White blood cells and immunological memory relate to formed elements including white cells (B.2.c) and lymphocytes (B.2.d).
Plant vascular systems including xylem and phloem address plant internal transport (B.4).
Fluid dynamics principles applied to biological systems relate to biodynamics of vertebrate circulation (B.3.e).
Hormonal regulation of water, salt, and calcium balance addresses excretory mechanisms (C.2).
Marine organism diversity relates to gases in the aquatic environment (A.2) and adaptation to special conditions (A.6).
Amphibian permeable skin serving gas exchange addresses vertebrate respiratory organs (A.3.b), freshwater-land adaptation (A.6), and vertebrate excretory systems (C.2.c).
Organ system formation during development addresses embryonic development of the circulatory system (B.3.d).
